behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from the recommendation results regardless of their relevance score.
behaviors.justification: A pure unit test of the ranker can miss failures where stock-state data is stale/misjoined/misapplied when catalog filtering and ranking are wired together. Real failure boundary is filter + catalog source + ranking pipeline integration, not OS/network.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local catalog store with mixed in-stock/out-of-stock products where out-of-stock items have the highest relevance features. Run the full recommendation assembly path and assert no out-of-stock product appears in output.

behaviors.description: The user preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings.
behaviors.justification: Mocking settings retrieval can hide schema/serialization/mapping bugs between user settings and filter logic. Minimum real failure boundary is module wiring across settings + filter + recommendation assembly.
behaviors.test_description: Create a user with blocked categories in the real local preferences store, run recommendation generation, and verify returned items contain no blocked categories even when those categories have high candidate scores.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm ranks products that match the user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: Ranking behavior depends on feature extraction and weighting across multiple modules; unit tests of scoring alone can miss failures in feature plumbing. No real LLM/non-deterministic model requirement is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Insert browsing events and candidate products into local stores so one group strongly matches recent browsing features and another has none. Execute end-to-end ranking pipeline and assert matching group is ordered above non-matching group under controlled tie-break inputs.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results, with stable ordering — requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if the underlying data hasn't changed.
behaviors.justification: Pagination stability often fails at query + sort + tie-breaker boundaries. Unit tests can miss nondeterministic ordering from data access layer behavior. No external OS/network dependency required.
behaviors.test_description: Populate local data with many similarly scored products and deterministic tie-break fields. Request page 2 twice through the full recommendation endpoint path with unchanged data and assert identical IDs and order each time.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding a filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: This is a business-rule constraint spanning candidate generation + diversification + ranking. Mocking components can hide real pipeline interactions that suppress novelty items. It does not require LLM stochastic judgment by itself.
behaviors.test_description: Set up user history concentrated in a narrow set of categories and a catalog with additional categories. Run full recommendation generation and assert at least one returned item belongs to an unseen category while core relevance constraints still hold.

behaviors.description: A new user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set.
behaviors.justification: Cold-start fallback depends on interaction between user-profile detection, popularity source, and ranking assembly. Unit tests can miss wiring bugs that return empty candidates.
behaviors.test_description: Create a user with no history and seed popularity metrics in the local store. Execute recommendation generation and assert non-empty output and that top items align with popularity ordering rules.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in the list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with.
behaviors.justification: Mocked tracking sinks can hide serialization/order/mapping bugs between recommendation response and event write path. Minimum boundary is API handler + event model + persistence integration.
behaviors.test_description: Serve a recommendation list, simulate user click events on specific positions, and verify persisted tracking records contain exact product ID, list position, and valid timestamp in the local event store.

behaviors.description: The response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: Latency under realistic load is sensitive to runtime, process scheduling, DB/index behavior, and I/O; in-process/mocked tests can falsely pass. Real failure boundary includes actual service process and local infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Run the recommendation service in a realistic test environment with a 100,000-product dataset in the actual local datastore/index, issue repeated requests for representative users, and assert p95 latency remains under 200ms after warm-up.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: This depends on event ingestion/state update feeding the ranking pipeline. Unit tests can miss failures in propagation from purchase records to feature state. No required external API/LLM boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations, record a purchase for a recommended item in the local transactions store, regenerate recommendations, and assert the purchased item's rank drops (or is removed) according to policy.

behaviors.description: The recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state — requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: Determinism failures usually come from unstable sorting, random seeds, clock usage, or read-path inconsistencies across modules. Unit tests can miss cross-module nondeterminism; no real LLM dependency implied.
behaviors.test_description: Freeze controllable time/random seed in test config, keep user and catalog state unchanged in local stores, call recommendation generation twice through the full stack, and assert identical ordered product IDs.
